question: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in each year from the year (within 2011-2016) that has the lowest teen birth rate for females ages 15-19 across all races in the United States through three years after that year, and also had 2020 Census populations under 100,000? List the city names in alphabetical order separated by `, `. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Filtering to Race or Hispanic Origin = "All Races" and Age Group = "15-19 Years" for 2011-2016, the minimum Birth Rate is 20.3 in 2016.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2011-2016 has the lowest teen birth rate for females ages 15-19 across all races in the United States? (Filter Race or Hispanic Origin == "All Races" and Age Group == "15-19 Years"; select the minimum Birth Rate.)

2.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer> release, 10 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, Edinburg, El Paso, Laredo, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, and San Antonio.
2.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer> release?

3.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer + 1> release, 12 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Baytown, Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, Edinburg, El Paso, Houston, Laredo, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, and San Antonio.
3.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer + 1> release?

4.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer + 2> release, 12 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, El Paso, Houston, Laredo, Longview, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, San Antonio, and Tyler.
4.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer + 2> release?

5.fact: In the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer + 3> release, 13 Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12%: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, Edinburg, El Paso, Laredo, Longview, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, San Antonio, Tyler, and Waco.
5.subquestion: Which Texas cities had diabetes prevalence above 12% in the CDC 500 Cities <node_1 answer + 3> release?

6.fact: According to the 2020 Census Redistricting Data for All Texas Places, the following 2020 Census populations (POP100 field) are recorded for the 9 cities from the intersection: Beaumont (115,282), Brownsville (186,738), Corpus Christi (317,863), El Paso (678,815), Laredo (255,205), McAllen (142,210), Mission (85,778), Pharr (79,715), and San Antonio (1,434,625). Only Mission and Pharr have populations under 100,000.
6.subquestion: Which cities from <intersection of nodes 2-5: Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, El Paso, Laredo, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, San Antonio> had population under 100,000 in the 2020 Census?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: Year with lowest teen birth rate for 15-19 all races (2011-2016) -> 2016
Derived year range: 2016-2019 (node_1 answer to node_1 answer + 3)
Node 2: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2016 release
Node 3: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2017 release
Node 4: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2018 release
Node 5: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2019 release
HOP 3 (d=1, data.gov filter - node 6 depends on hop 2):
Node 6: 2020 Census populations for the 9 cities -> under 100,000: Mission, Pharr
